notes.anchor: on the day before the Ides of April
notes.note: 12 April 53 BC; written from Cicero's villa near Cumae, the day after the previous note to Tiro (16.14).

notes.anchor: I received your note in a tottering little hand
notes.note: Cicero affectionately reads the shakiness of Tiro's handwriting as proof of how gravely ill his secretary still is.

notes.anchor: and a cook along with him
notes.note: Cicero sends one of his own kitchen slaves so that the convalescent Tiro can be properly fed; the cook is unnamed.
